city_report.transport: Informazioni sul trasporto pubblico in aggiornamento.

attraction.name: Hallgrimskirkja
attraction.description: Iconic Lutheran church inspired by basalt columns.

attraction.name: Harpa
attraction.description: Modern concert hall and conference center.

attraction.name: Sun Voyager
attraction.description: Steel sculpture of a boat located on the waterfront.

attraction.name: Perlan
attraction.description: Landmark building with a museum and revolving restaurant.

attraction.name: Laugavegur
attraction.description: The main shopping and nightlife street of Reykjavik.

attraction.name: National Museum
attraction.description: Museum showcasing Iceland's history from the settlement.

attraction.name: Tjörnin Lake
attraction.description: Quiet lake in the city center popular for bird watching.

attraction.name: Settlement Exhibition
attraction.description: Museum based on a 10th-century Viking longhouse.

attraction.name: Blue Lagoon (Bus)
attraction.description: Geothermal spa (reached by bus from the capital).

attraction.name: Imagine Peace Tower
attraction.description: Memorial to John Lennon on Viðey Island.

city_report.description: La capitale più settentrionale del mondo è un laboratorio di energia geotermica e sicurezza assoluta. Piccola e pulita, Reykjavik offre un'esperienza di vita a stretto contatto con una natura incontaminata.
city_report.wiki_intro: Reykjavík is the capital of and largest city in Iceland and with an urban area population of around 233,000 (2019), it is the home to two-thirds of Iceland's population. It is the centre of culture and life of the Icelandic people, and is one of the focal points of tourism in Iceland. The city is spread out, and has sprawling suburbs. The city centre, however, is a very small area characterised...
